questions.stem:
A Vertex AI Pipelines workflow records executions, artifacts, and parameters for a model training run. The team needs lineage in Vertex ML Metadata so they can trace which dataset and component produced the approved model.

Which option should the practitioner choose?

options.text: Configure Vertex ML Metadata so the team can meet the low-latency serving requirement without moving the decision into an unrelated service. for `shipment_milestones`
Rely on a ad hoc notebook copy instead of configuring Vertex ML Metadata for `shipment_milestones`
Rely on a nightly full rebuild instead of configuring Vertex ML Metadata for `shipment_milestones`
Rely on a dashboard-only filter instead of configuring Vertex ML Metadata for `shipment_milestones`

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `Vertex ML Metadata` is the documented Vertex ML Metadata capability tied to this scenario. It addresses low-latency serving without replacing the platform workflow with a manual workaround.

**B** is incorrect because the ad hoc notebook copy breaks lineage or repeatability when the workflow is rerun; it would not exercise the documented `Vertex ML Metadata` behavior needed here.

**C** is incorrect because the nightly full rebuild moves the control outside the certification service being tested; it would not exercise the documented `Vertex ML Metadata` behavior needed here.

**D** is incorrect because the dashboard-only filter does not expose the configuration point required by the scenario; it would not exercise the documented `Vertex ML Metadata` behavior needed here.

See [Vertex ML Metadata](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/ml-metadata/introduction) for the supporting documentation.
